data: red(1599)+white(4898)=6497 from pinned GitHub mirror (OpenML blocked)
metric: RelMSE (no intercept in metric; intercept here means a design column)
note: OLS RelMSE is invariant to per-column scaling, so the gap is driven by the design columns (intercept / type), not standardization.

variants.variant: E5 baseline: 11 chem + type, no intercept
variants.comment: matches paper d=12 (this is the current E5 encoding)
alt d=12 (intercept replaces the type indicator)

resolution: aligned canonical red+white CSVs give OLS 0.0156 / BinAgg 0.021 (mu=1), matching the paper's 0.016 / 0.022; the earlier E5 value 0.027 is attributed to the data source / red-white feature-column alignment, not to a preprocessing-column or scaling choice (all design variants give ~0.0156).
